summary: MCP terminal-schedule guard faithfully mirrors HTTP UpdateTaskStatus:147-160 (short-circuit order, master_id||source_id, task_masters lookup, isRollingMaster). No BLOCK/WARN. zoe item is pre-existing cross-path, out of scope.

findings.claim: Unscheduled rolling instance marked terminal returns null (allow) then violates DB CHECK chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled (raw throw); whether the exemption should backfill scheduled_at is unaddressed.
findings.evidence: Helper returns null on rolling match (tasks.js:81). HTTP UpdateTaskStatus.js sets scheduled_at only on custom completedAt (L190) or isFutureScheduled cancel/skip (L199-200) — neither fires for an unscheduled rolling instance, so HTTP hits the SAME CHECK. Recorded decision brain:fact#79254 sanctions the exemption allowing terminal-without-schedule. Behavior is identical on both paths and pre-exists this leg; the leg's goal is parity, which is achieved.
findings.fix: Out of scope for this parity leg. File a separate backlog item to reconcile fact#79254 exemption with the DB CHECK constraint on BOTH HTTP and MCP paths.

findings.claim: _willBeScheduled counts existing.scheduled_at, fields.scheduledAt, fields.date but not fields.time; this is correct parity and correct logic.
findings.evidence: HTTP guard (UpdateTaskStatus.js:155) considers only existing.scheduled_at + body.scheduledAt. A time-only update with no date and no existing scheduled_at cannot produce a complete scheduled_at, so the conservative block is the intended outcome. MCP is a superset of HTTP (adds fields.date). No false block on a row that would actually be scheduled.

findings.claim: set_task_status accepts status: z.string() and lacks HTTP's VALID_STATUSES check + 'missed' system-only 403; pre-existing, separate from this leg's guard.
findings.evidence: UpdateTaskStatus.js:104-109 validates status and rejects 'missed'. MCP set_task_status has neither. This is a distinct guard from the terminal-schedule guard added here; TERMINAL_REQUIRES_SCHEDULE correctly excludes 'missed' exactly as HTTP does. Not introduced by this change.
findings.fix: Out of scope; optionally backlog a separate MCP status-validation parity item.
